opening_remarks:
GOLDMAN SACHS STATEMENT ON STRESS CAPITAL BUFFER 

Firm announces 33% increase in common stock dividend 

NEW YORK, NY, JulyВ 1, 2025 В— On Friday, JuneВ 27, the Federal Reserve released the results of its 2025 Comprehensive Capital
Analysis and Review (В“CCARВ”) stress test process. Goldman Sachs expects the firmВ’s Stress Capital Buffer (В“SCBВ”) requirement will be 3.4%, resulting in a Standardized Common Equity Tier 1 (В“CET1В”) ratio requirement
of 10.9%, effective October 1. 
 
The Federal Reserve will provide the firmВ’s final SCB requirement by August 31. These results and
effective date may be subject to further changes pending the finalization of the Federal ReserveВ’s outstanding proposal on SCB averaging.
qa_section:
In addition, the Federal Reserve disclosed that the firmВ’s current SCB, from the CCAR 2024 test, has been reduced by 10В basis points
to 6.1%. This results in a current CET1 ratio requirement of 13.6%, effective immediately. 
 
The firmВ’s capital plan includes a 33%
increase in the common stock dividend from $3.00 to $4.00 per share beginning JulyВ 1, 2025, subject to approval by the firmВ’s Board of Directors at the customary third quarter meeting. This increase is a continuation of the firmВ’s
plan to pay shareholders a sustainable and growing dividend. 
 
В“TodayВ’s announcement is a reflection of the work we have done
over the years to reduce our capital intensity,В” said Chairman and CEO David Solomon. В“The Federal Reserve has expressed its intention to institute a more transparent and fair approach to these tests, as it looks to uphold the safety and
soundness of our financial system. A more balanced approach to the tests would allow Goldman Sachs to continue to serve our clientsВ’ needs, invest in our world-class businesses, and support economic growth. We look forward to continued
progress.В” 
 
### 
 
Goldman Sachs is a
leading global financial institution that delivers a broad range of financial services to a large and diversified client base that includes corporations, financial institutions, governments and individuals. Founded in 1869, the firm is headquartered
in NewВ York and maintains offices in all major financial centers around the world. 
 
Cautionary Note on Forward-Looking Statements 

This press release includes В“forward-looking statementsВ” within the meaning of the safe harbor provisions of the U.S. Private Securities Litigation
Reform Act of 1995. Forward-looking statements are not historical facts, but instead represent only the firmВ’s beliefs regarding future events, many of which, by their nature, are inherently uncertain and outside the firmВ’s control. It is
possible that the firmВ’s final Stress Capital Buffer and capital actions (including dividends) may differ, possibly materially, from those described in this press release. For a discussion of some of the risks and important factors that could
affect the firmВ’s future results and financial condition, as well as its actual Stress Capital Buffer and capital actions, see В“Risk FactorsВ” in Part I, Item 1A of the firmВ’s Annual Report on Form

10-K
 for the year ended DecemberВ 31, 2024.
